text.title: internationalization Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for internationalization. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use stable dotted keys (feature.surface.intent) for all user-facing strings; never auto-generate hash keys or use the EN string as the key.
rule.rationale: Stable keys survive copy edits; hash keys change when content changes, invalidating all translations. String-as-key forces re-translation on wording tweaks.

rule.statement: Use ICU MessageFormat for all plurals and gender/select — never gettext-style ngettext for new code; emit all CLDR-required plural categories for the target locale.
rule.rationale: EN has only one/other; Ukrainian requires one/few/many/other; Arabic requires zero/one/two/few/many/other. Missing categories produce wrong strings for specific numbers.

rule.statement: Use pybabel extract to pull strings into messages.pot; compile to binary .mo files before deployment — gettext only reads compiled catalogues.
rule.rationale: Shipping uncompiled .po files means the translation layer silently falls back to EN; the compile step must be part of the build, not manual.

rule.statement: Detect locale from request in priority order: query param, Accept-Language header, user preference; store in a ContextVar so all downstream code reads the correct locale without passing it explicitly.
rule.rationale: Passing locale as a function parameter through every layer is fragile and verbose; ContextVar provides an implicit request-scoped locale that is safe for async frameworks.

rule.statement: Always format dates, numbers, and currencies through Intl.DateTimeFormat / Intl.NumberFormat (JS) or babel.dates / babel.numbers (Python) — never via string concatenation or manual separator insertion.
rule.rationale: Decimal separators differ (1,000 US vs 1.000 DE vs 1 000 FR vs ١٬٠٠٠ AR); calendar systems differ (Gregorian vs Hijri). Manual formatting produces wrong output for non-EN locales.

rule.statement: Use logical CSS properties (margin-inline-start, padding-block, text-align: start, border-inline-start) instead of physical properties (margin-left, padding-top, text-align: left) in all new UI components.
rule.rationale: Physical properties do not flip for RTL languages; logical properties automatically map to the correct physical direction based on writing-mode and dir attributes.

rule.statement: Set dir="rtl" at the root element for RTL locales; use dir="ltr" on code blocks, phone numbers, and other LTR-only content within RTL pages.
rule.rationale: Without dir="ltr" on code blocks, browsers apply RTL text layout to code, mangling indentation and operator reading order.

rule.statement: Treat the EN catalogue as the source of truth; push only EN to the TMS and pull only translations back — never allow the TMS to overwrite EN entries.
rule.rationale: Bidirectional sync can clobber human-reviewed EN copy with TMS auto-suggestions. One-directional push preserves EN as the authoritative source.

rule.statement: Fail the build if any locale's key set differs from EN; check that ICU placeholder names match exactly across all locales.
rule.rationale: A missing key silently falls back to EN; a renamed placeholder ({userName} vs {ім'яКористувача}) produces a broken string with the literal placeholder name visible.

rule.statement: Provide a description or @context comment for every ambiguous string; include a screenshot or UI description — "Open" as a verb and "Open" as an adjective translate differently in most languages.
rule.rationale: Context-free strings cause polysemy errors. For "Open" in Japanese, the wrong word choice is over 50% likely without UI context.

rule.statement: Run pseudo-localization (replace characters with accented variants like Hello → Ḫèĺĺō) during development to surface unwrapped strings, fixed widths, and RTL bleeds before real translation.
rule.rationale: Pseudo-localization catches i18n gaps cheaply in dev; real translation is expensive and slow. Finding a hardcoded string in pseudo-locale review costs minutes; finding it post-launch costs weeks.

rule.statement: LLM translation quality is non-uniform: strong on EN/ES/DE/FR/UK, weak on low-resource locales (Hausa, Tagalog, Khmer).
rule.rationale: For low-resource locales, professional human translation is essential; LLM output is unreliable.

rule.statement: Plural rules: LLMs often emit only one/other and miss few/many/two for Slavic, Arabic, Welsh.
rule.rationale: CLDR plural categories must be verified against official rules; LLMs hallucinate category completeness.

rule.statement: Context loss: same EN string ("Open") reused as verb and adjective will be mistranslated without screenshots / @context comments.
rule.rationale: Polysemy requires context; isolated strings produce wrong translations 50%+ of the time.

rule.statement: Concatenation refactors: agents may turn "Hello " + name into f"Hello {name}" instead of an ICU template — they must use {name} placeholders inside the message string.
rule.rationale: String interpolation at the call site prevents translators from reordering words for grammar; ICU templates allow word order changes.

rule.statement: RTL: agents tend to flip layouts globally instead of relying on logical CSS properties; can break LTR rendering.
rule.rationale: Logical CSS handles bidirectional automatically; manual flips break LTR content like code blocks.

rule.statement: Date/number formatting: agents fall back to toLocaleString() without specifying calendar, numbering system, or hourCycle.
rule.rationale: Explicit Intl options ensure consistent formatting across browsers and prevent ambiguous dates.

rule.statement: Key collisions across modules when no namespace convention exists.
rule.rationale: Dotted keys (feature.surface.intent) prevent collisions and enable modular key management.

rule.statement: Two-pass agent workflow: pass 1 extracts and externalizes strings; pass 2 validates plural categories, placeholder matching, and Intl usage.
rule.rationale: Separation of concerns reduces risk; each pass can be reviewed and tested independently.

rule.statement: Use ICU MessageFormat from day one — gettext-style _n cannot express gender/select cleanly.
rule.rationale: ICU is strictly more expressive; starting with it avoids costly migration later.

rule.statement: Stable dotted keys (feature.surface.intent) survive copy edits; auto-generated hash keys do not.
rule.rationale: Copy changes invalidate hash-based keys; stable keys preserve translation investment.

rule.statement: Reserve 30-50% layout headroom — DE/RU/UK strings expand significantly vs EN.
rule.rationale: Fixed widths break on expansion; flexbox with headroom prevents truncation.

rule.statement: Use logical CSS properties (margin-inline-start, padding-block) and dir="auto" on neutral content; gate manual overrides behind [dir="rtl"].
rule.rationale: Logical properties are future-proof; manual overrides are fragile and hard to test.

rule.statement: Format dates/numbers/currency through Intl (JS) or Babel (Python), never manual string concat — locale-specific separators differ.
rule.rationale: Intl handles all formatting edge cases; manual concatenation is error-prone.

rule.statement: Treat the EN catalogue as source of truth, version it in Git, and make the TMS a one-way sync target except for translations.
rule.rationale: EN is the reference; TMS is a delivery channel. One-way sync prevents confusion and clobbering.

rule.statement: Validate on every PR: every key in EN exists in every shipped locale; ICU placeholders match across locales.
rule.rationale: Build-time validation catches drift before users see broken strings.
